meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop building demos and start generating revenue. Learn to implement SaaS pricing, escape tutorial hell, and launch your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Escaping tutorial hell requires shifting focus from code perfection to business validation. Implement a subscription model early using Stripe and Antigravity to force market feedback. By building a functional payment flow, you transform your project from a static demo into a monetizable SaaS asset that solves real user pain.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks in the trenches of 'tutorial hell,' spinning up endless boilerplate code that leads nowhere. Your codebase is a graveyard of abandoned features, and your confidence is eroding under the weight of infinite 'how-to' videos that never mention the one thing that actually matters: revenue.

The difference between a hobbyist project and a professional SaaS is the payment gate. Once you charge your first dollar, the stakes change, the feedback loop tightens, and you stop being a student of code—you become a builder of value. This guide strips away the fluff to help you implement a robust pricing engine and launch your MVP into the wild.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify your unit of value. Are you charging per seat, per API call, or per project? This defines your database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Define a scalable database schema for a subscription-based product that supports three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for usage limits and subscription status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Prisma schema for SaaS subscription tiers'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Don't build your own billing system. Use Stripe's infrastructure to handle secure payments, webhooks, and subscription lifecycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler in TypeScript that updates user subscription status in the database when a checkout session succeeds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Configure Stripe webhooks and secure API routes for billing'

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing page that highlights the 'Pro' tier as the default choice to optimize for your primary revenue goal.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive pricing component using Tailwind CSS. Include a toggle for monthly vs yearly billing and a 'Best Value' badge on the Pro tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude build 'Create pricing.tsx component with Tailwind'

steps.title: Implement Access Control Middleware
steps.description: Protect your premium features with middleware that checks the user's subscription status before rendering sensitive components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js middleware that verifies user subscription status. Redirect unauthorized users to the pricing page if they try to access /dashboard/pro-features.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship your code to a production environment. Your goal is not perfection, but the ability for a user to enter a credit card.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment checklist for a SaaS MVP, including environment variable management and production database migration steps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify production readiness and generate deployment docs'

pro_tips: Always price based on the value provided to the customer, not the cost of your server resources.
Use a 'Free Trial' rather than a 'Free Tier' to avoid attracting users who have no intention of ever paying.
Automate your dunning process—failed payments are revenue leaks that kill early-stage SaaS growth.
Keep your pricing page simple; too many choices lead to decision paralysis and zero conversions.
Implement 'One-Click Upgrades' inside your dashboard to reduce friction for power users wanting more limits.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus to distribution. Reach out to 50 potential users on Twitter or LinkedIn, offer them a lifetime discount, and collect feedback to iterate on your core value proposition.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to stop learning and start earning. Build the product, secure the revenue, and escape the loop of endless tutorials. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
